_license: Public health, pharma, food-safety + medical records — DOJ press releases, DEA admin court, FDA openFDA, CMS provider data, USDA FSIS, CDC public reports, Leapfrog Hospital Safety Grade, Center for Science in the Public Interest, Public Citizen Health Research Group, Truth Initiative. Cite original source URLs.

entries.title: Biogen pays $900M to resolve FCA allegations of kickbacks to neurologists
entries.summary: DOJ False Claims Act settlement: Biogen allegedly paid speaker-program kickbacks to induce prescriptions of MS drugs Avonex/Tysabri/Tecfidera.

entries.title: Mallinckrodt $260M FCA settlement — H.P. Acthar Gel kickbacks + Medicaid underpayment
entries.summary: DOJ alleged Mallinckrodt paid kickbacks via the Chronic Disease Fund to mask Acthar's price + underpaid Medicaid rebates.

entries.title: Indivior $600M global resolution over Suboxone film marketing
entries.summary: DOJ: Indivior misled prescribers about Suboxone film's risk to children + caused false Medicaid claims.

entries.title: Novartis $729M to resolve speaker-program kickback FCA case
entries.summary: DOJ alleged Novartis ran sham speaker programs as cardiovascular / diabetes prescriber kickbacks.

entries.title: Gilead $97M FCA settlement — Letairis copay-charity kickback
entries.summary: DOJ: Gilead used a copay charity to steer Letairis patients + induce Medicare prescriptions.

entries.title: Walgreens $106.8M FCA settlement — billing for never-dispensed prescriptions
entries.summary: DOJ: Walgreens billed Medicare/Medicaid/Tricare for prescriptions it processed but never actually dispensed to patients (2009-2020).

entries.title: CVS / Omnicare $18M FCA case — long-term-care kickbacks
entries.summary: DOJ alleged Omnicare (CVS) dispensed drugs without valid prescriptions for nursing-home residents.

entries.title: HCA-affiliate physician practice $60M FCA settlement (oncology billing)
entries.summary: DOJ: HCA-affiliated oncology clinic billed for medically-unnecessary infusions + unlicensed nurses.

entries.title: Centene multistate $165M+ Medicaid PBM settlement
entries.summary: Centene paid 22+ states settlements over overbilling Medicaid programs through pharmacy-benefit-manager rebates.

entries.title: Booz Allen $377.5M FCA settlement — commercial cost mischarging
entries.summary: DOJ: BAH improperly charged commercial costs to federal contracts incl. HHS/CMS work.

entries.title: Cigna $172.3M FCA settlement — Medicare Advantage risk-adjustment fraud
entries.summary: DOJ: Cigna submitted inaccurate diagnosis codes to inflate Medicare Advantage risk-adjustment payments (2014-2019).

entries.title: Humana $90M FCA settlement — Medicare Part D bid-process misrepresentation
entries.summary: DOJ: Humana misrepresented expected bid costs in Medicare Part D prescription-drug-plan bids.

entries.title: McKesson $150M DEA civil penalty — failure to report suspicious opioid orders
entries.summary: DEA: McKesson failed to design + operate effective suspicious-order monitoring across 12 states.

entries.title: Cardinal Health $44M DEA settlement — opioid distribution diversion controls
entries.summary: DEA: Cardinal Health failed to maintain effective controls against diversion of controlled substances.

entries.title: AmerisourceBergen $16M DEA + state settlements — opioid distribution
entries.summary: DEA + state AG cases: AmerisourceBergen failed to identify + report suspicious orders.

entries.title: CVS pharmacies $5M DEA civil penalty — controlled-substance dispensing recordkeeping
entries.summary: DEA: CVS pharmacies failed to keep accurate records of controlled-substance receipts + dispensing.

entries.title: Walmart pharmacies — DOJ/DEA civil filings over opioid prescription handling
entries.summary: DOJ/DEA filed civil claims alleging Walmart's mail-order + retail pharmacies filled invalid opioid prescriptions; settled portions of program.

entries.title: Walgreens $80M DEA settlement — historic opioid distribution lapses
entries.summary: DEA: Walgreens distribution centers (notably Jupiter, FL) failed to design + operate suspicious-order systems.

entries.title: Mallinckrodt $35M DEA settlement — failure to report suspicious oxycodone orders
entries.summary: DEA: Mallinckrodt sold ~500M oxycodone tabs in Florida pill-mill region without flagging suspicious orders.

entries.title: J&J — national opioid settlement (~$5B over 9 years)
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: J&J among manufacturers paying state/local governments for opioid-crisis harms.

entries.title: McKesson — Big-3 distributor opioid settlement (~$7.9B)
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: McKesson share of $26B Big-3 distributor agreement (with Cardinal + ABC).

entries.title: AmerisourceBergen — Big-3 distributor opioid settlement
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: ABC share of $26B Big-3 distributor agreement.

entries.title: Cardinal Health — Big-3 distributor opioid settlement
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: Cardinal share of $26B Big-3 distributor agreement.

entries.title: CVS opioid settlement (~$5B over 10 years)
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: CVS pharmacies tier.

entries.title: Walgreens opioid settlement (~$5.7B over 15 years)
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: Walgreens pharmacies tier.

entries.title: Walmart opioid settlement (~$3.1B)
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: Walmart pharmacies tier.

entries.title: Teva $4.25B opioid settlement (cash + naloxone supply)
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: Teva manufacturer tier — cash + ~$1.2B in donated Narcan.

entries.title: Allergan / AbbVie $2.37B opioid settlement
entries.summary: National Opioid Settlement: Allergan (AbbVie) manufacturer tier.

entries.title: Endo $600M+ opioid settlement (Ch. 11 reorganization)
entries.summary: Endo emerged from bankruptcy after settling opioid claims with US states + DOJ.

entries.title: Leapfrog Spring 2025: HCA chain mixed grades
entries.summary: Leapfrog: HCA-affiliated hospitals collectively grade B median, with notable C/D-graded facilities in multiple states.

entries.title: Leapfrog Spring 2025: Tenet chain mixed grades
entries.summary: Leapfrog: Tenet hospitals collectively grade B/C median with multiple D-graded facilities across TX/FL/AZ.

entries.title: Leapfrog Spring 2025: CHS chain — multiple D/F grades
entries.summary: Leapfrog: Community Health Systems facilities concentrated in C/D bands; several D-graded hospitals in published roster.

entries.title: Leapfrog Spring 2025: UHS chain mixed grades
entries.summary: Leapfrog: UHS facilities split across A-D bands; behavioral-health hospitals not graded.

entries.title: Leapfrog Spring 2025: Mayo Clinic — Grade A across reporting hospitals
entries.summary: Leapfrog: Mayo Clinic facilities consistently A-graded across Rochester, Phoenix, Jacksonville locations.

entries.title: Leapfrog Spring 2025: Cleveland Clinic — Grade A
entries.summary: Leapfrog: Cleveland Clinic main campus + most satellite hospitals A-graded.

entries.title: CMS Nursing Home Compare: Genesis Healthcare — below-average chain
entries.summary: CMS overall star ratings for Genesis-operated facilities cluster in 1-2 star band per published facility roster (2024 data).

entries.title: CMS Nursing Home Compare: Brookdale SNF subset — mixed
entries.summary: CMS: Brookdale skilled-nursing facilities (subset of portfolio) cluster 2-3 stars overall.

entries.title: CMS Nursing Home Compare: Ensign Group — above-average chain
entries.summary: CMS: Ensign-operated facilities cluster in 3-4 star band, with health-inspection ratings outperforming national median.

entries.title: CMS Nursing Home Compare: ProMedica/ManorCare — mixed grades
entries.summary: CMS: ProMedica skilled-nursing portfolio (formerly HCR ManorCare) clusters 2-3 stars.

entries.title: FSIS Class-I recalls — multiple in past 24 months (foreign material, Listeria, undeclared allergen)
entries.summary: USDA FSIS: Tyson-related recalls in 2023-2024 include chicken breast strips (metal), ground beef (E. coli concerns), and ready-to-eat items.

entries.title: FSIS Class-I recall — 7M+ lbs deli meat (Jarratt, VA) tied to multi-state Listeria outbreak (9 deaths)
entries.summary: USDA FSIS + CDC: Boar's Head Jarratt VA plant recalled all ready-to-eat deli products after Listeria outbreak killed 9, hospitalized 57 across 18 states (July-Sep 2024).

entries.title: FSIS Class-I recalls — Banquet chicken strips (plastic) + ready-to-eat items
entries.summary: USDA FSIS: Conagra-operated Banquet chicken strip recall (foreign material) + multiple sub-brand RTE recalls in 2023-2024.

entries.title: FSIS Class-I recall — 11.7M lbs RTE meat + poultry (Durant OK) Listeria
entries.summary: USDA FSIS: BrucePac October 2024 — one of the largest USDA recalls ever; affected products distributed to ~75 brand customers nationwide.

entries.title: FSIS Class-I recall — Perdue chicken nuggets/tenders (metal wire)
entries.summary: USDA FSIS: Perdue Foods 2024 recall of frozen ready-to-eat chicken products for possible metal-wire contamination.

entries.title: FSIS Class-I recalls — JBS / Swift ground beef (E. coli) multiple events
entries.summary: USDA FSIS: JBS-operated processors issued multiple ground-beef recalls for possible E. coli O157:H7 in 2023-2024.

entries.title: CDC AR Threats: Tyson reintroduced antibiotics in chicken (2023)
entries.summary: CDC + public reporting: Tyson reversed its 'No Antibiotics Ever' chicken program in 2023, reintroducing 'ionophore' antibiotics — though those aren't medically important to humans.

entries.title: CDC AR Threats: Perdue antibiotic-free chicken leader
entries.summary: CDC + USDA: Perdue maintains industry-leading 'No Antibiotics Ever' protocol across hatchery + grow-out for fresh chicken.

entries.title: CDC AR Threats: JBS — high use of medically-important antibiotics in cattle
entries.summary: CDC + Pew/NRDC analysis: JBS feedlot operations rank among the largest US users of medically-important antibiotics in beef cattle.

entries.title: CDC AR Threats: Cargill — mid-tier antibiotic stewardship
entries.summary: CDC + industry reporting: Cargill reports declining cephalosporin use but continues medically-important antibiotic use in cattle finishing.

entries.title: CDC AR Threats: Chick-fil-A backed off 'No Antibiotics Ever' in 2024
entries.summary: Chick-fil-A March 2024 announced shift from 'No Antibiotics Ever' to 'No Antibiotics Important to Human Medicine' — a category broader than CDC's strictest standard.

entries.title: CSPI Xtreme Eating: Cheesecake Factory — recurring honors
entries.summary: CSPI Xtreme Eating Awards: Cheesecake Factory items routinely cited for 2,000+ calorie single servings, including Linda's Fudge Cake (1,610 cal) + Pasta Napoletana (2,310 cal).

entries.title: CSPI Xtreme Eating: Applebee's classic combos cited
entries.summary: CSPI: Applebee's Bourbon Street Steak + Shrimp 'n Parmesan Sirloin among cited items for sodium + saturated-fat content.

entries.title: CSPI Xtreme Eating: Buffalo Wild Wings — high-calorie shareables
entries.summary: CSPI: Buffalo Wild Wings cheese-curd burger + cheesy-bread combos cited for 2,500+ calorie meals.

entries.title: CSPI Xtreme Eating: Texas Roadhouse cited
entries.summary: CSPI: Texas Roadhouse Cactus Blossom appetizer + multiple steak combos cited for high sodium + saturated-fat.

entries.title: CSPI: Sonic shake-line label-misleading callout
entries.summary: CSPI: Sonic shake / blast desserts marketed as snacks but routinely exceed 1,000 calories.

entries.title: Public Citizen 'Do Not Use' list — Chantix (varenicline) flagged
entries.summary: Public Citizen Health Research Group: Chantix on 'Do Not Use' list citing neuropsychiatric + cardiovascular adverse events.

entries.title: Public Citizen 'Do Not Use' list — Avandia (rosiglitazone) — historic
entries.summary: Public Citizen Health Research Group: Avandia long on 'Do Not Use' list over cardiovascular risk; FDA restrictions retained.

entries.title: Public Citizen 'Do Not Use' — Pradaxa monitoring concerns flagged
entries.summary: Public Citizen Health Research Group: long-running concerns flagged for Pradaxa bleeding-risk profile.

entries.title: Public Citizen — Xarelto (rivaroxaban) bleeding-risk flagged
entries.summary: Public Citizen Health Research Group: Xarelto flagged for bleeding events vs. alternatives w/ established reversal agents.

entries.title: Truth Initiative: Altria — historic youth-marketing + Juul investment
entries.summary: Truth Initiative + FDA + state AG: Altria's $12.8B Juul investment (2018, mostly written down) implicated in youth-vaping epidemic; Altria divested in 2023.

entries.title: Truth Initiative + multistate: Juul $1.7B settlements over youth marketing
entries.summary: Truth Initiative + state AGs: Juul paid $1.7B+ across multistate settlements (NC, multistate coalition) over targeting minors.

entries.title: Truth Initiative: BAT — kid-flavor Vuse / R.J. Reynolds menthol concerns
entries.summary: Truth Initiative: BAT (R.J. Reynolds) Vuse e-cig + menthol-cigarette portfolio cited for youth + menthol-targeted-community marketing.

entries.title: Truth Initiative: PMI heat-not-burn marketing concerns
entries.summary: Truth Initiative: PMI's IQOS heat-not-burn marketing claims contested as misleading on harm reduction.
